name: A class of error-estimating methods
statement_text:
If (333b) has order p and (333a) has order p + 1 and
B = bs+1 , then

Conversely, if (333d) holds with cs = 1 and B = 0 and, in addition,

then (333b) has order p and (333a) has order p + 1.
proof_text:
Proof. For a given tree t, let Φ(t) denote the elementary weight for (333a) and
Φ(t) the elementary weight for (333b). Because the latter method has order
p, it follows that for a tree t = [t1 t2 · · · tm ], with order not exceeding p + 1, we
have Φ(ti ) = 1/γ(ti ), for i = 1, 2, . . . , m. Hence, for a method identical with
(333a) except for b replaced by the basis vector es+1 , the elementary weight
corresponding to t will be

                                             m
                                                   1      r(t)
                                                        =      .
                                             i=1
                                                 γ(ti )   γ(t)

Adding B multiplied by this quantity to Φ(t) gives the result
                                                 r(t)          1
                                  Φ(t) + B            =
                                                       Φ(t) =      ,
                                                 γ(t)         γ(t)
which is equivalent to (333d).
   To prove the converse, we first note that, because B = 0, the previous
argument can be reversed. That is, if (333b) has order p then (333d) implies
that (333a) has order p + 1. Hence, it is only necessary to prove that (333b)
has order p. We calculate Φ(t), for r(t) ≤ p as follows, where we have written
χi (t) for the coefficient of bi in Φ(t)

variables.name: B
variables.definition: bs+1, the coefficient for the additional stage in the embedded method pair

variables.name: Φ(t)
variables.definition: elementary weights computed using the artificial tableau (333c)

variables.name: r(t)
variables.definition: order of the tree t, with r(t) ≤ p+1

variables.name: γ(t)
variables.definition: density of the tree t (standard in Butcher tree theory)

variables.name: cs
variables.definition: the last abscissa in the Runge-Kutta method (333b)

variables.name: bs+1
variables.definition: coefficient for the (s+1)-th stage in the full method (333a)

variables.name: as+1,j
variables.definition: Runge-Kutta matrix entries for the (s+1)-th stage, j=1,...,s

variables.name: bj
variables.definition: weights for the output in method (333b), j=1,...,s

variables.name: aij
variables.definition: Runge-Kutta matrix entries for stages i,j in method (333b)

equations.content: the full (s+1)-stage method with order p+1
the s-stage method with order p
artificial tableau used to compute elementary weights Φ(t)

preamble: The theorem concerns an embedded Runge-Kutta method pair: (333b) is an s-stage method of order p, and (333a) is an (s+1)-stage method of order p+1, where the additional stage reuses derivative calculations. The artificial tableau (333c) is introduced to simplify order conditions, with B = bs+1 and elementary weights Φ(t).
